contentBlocks.original:
हरे मुरारे मधुकैटभारे गोपाल गोविंद मुकुंद शौरे ।
यज्ञेश नारायण कृष्ण विष्णो निराश्रयं माम् जगदीश रक्षः ||
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hare murāre madhukaiṭabhāre gopāla goviṃda mukuṃda śaure .
yajñeśa nārāyaṇa kṛṣṇa viṣṇo nirāśrayaṃ mām jagadīśa rakṣaḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation: Murari, the enemy of Madhu and Kaitabha, Gopala, Govinda, Mukunda, the crowning lord, the lord of sacrifices, Narayana, Krishna, Vishnu, O lord of the universe, protect me, who has no other resort.
contentBlocks.meanings: हरे (hare) – O Hari; मुरारे (murāre) – enemy of Mura; मधुकैटभारे (madhukaiṭabhāre) – enemy of Madhu and Kaitabha (madhu:Madhu | kaiṭabha:Kaitabha | āre:enemy); गोपाल (gopāla) – protector of cows; गोविंद (goviṃda) – Govinda; मुकुंद (mukuṃda) – giver of liberation; शौरे (śaure) – descendant of Śūra; यज्ञेश (yajñeśa) – lord of sacrifices (yajña:sacrifice | īśa:lord); नारायण (nārāyaṇa) – Narayana; कृष्ण (kṛṣṇa) – Krishna; विष्णो (viṣṇo) – O Vishnu; निराश्रयं (nirāśrayaṃ) – without refuge; माम् (mām) – me; जगदीश (jagadīśa) – lord of the universe (jagat:universe | īśa:lord); रक्षः (rakṣaḥ) – protect;
